title: No limitations when starting multiple machines with WSL
body:
### Issue Description

Not sure if it's a bug or something that should work this way.
In Podman Desktop we noticed that Mac users can only start one machine while on Windows there is no limitations. 
is it done on purpose?

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. start two or more machines on Windows
2. no error displayed


### Describe the results you received

N/A

### Describe the results you expected

I would expect to see an error message when starting a machine if another one is already running

comments.body:
I don't see a reason why you shouldn't be allowed to run more than one VM at the same time. I am not sure why it is limited on macos.
@baude @ashley-cui PTAL

That's by design, it doesn't make sense to run more than one VM at a time since you would have to either specify the connection every time you run a podman command or constantly change connections. At that point, it's probably easier to just boot up the correct machine. I think windows should be fixed to only allow one VM, as that's what happens on Mac and Linux

But what is that problem with specifying the connection every time? I see no problem with allowing this as a use case. Consider something like one machine per project you are working on. I could easily set the connection env var in my shell and then have two windows for both projects were you could work in parallel.

General speaking changing this now for windows is a breaking change as existing users relying on multiple VMs at once will be broken if you start blocking this.

I think we should allow multiple machines as well, especially as we move towards supporting multiple Arches.
On a M2 MAC, I could have a machine running Arm and another running X86, then I would want to build an image for both arches without using qemu-user-static.
we cannot allow multiple machines without retooling a number of things, some of which we do not own.  if we need to do this, then we need create an epic and get all the things gathered up to do so.
I'll fix up windows to match Mac/Linux behavior for consistency (I'd argue this is a bug) and if we want, we can change this later as an epic.

I disagree, if it worked before on windows it is a breaking change. It can break people. If it works there is no reason to set arbitrary limits.

> we cannot allow multiple machines without retooling a number of things, some of which we do not own. if we need to do this, then we need create an epic and get all the things gathered up to do so.

Can you expand on that? What tooling?
It's in our docs, so I'd still see it as a bugfix: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2c6c5d4becfb049c6acf11f5aa0cbe69581539ae/docs/source/markdown/podman-machine-start.1.md?plain=1#L23
But what is the bug? Taking away functionality for no benefit doesn't sound like a good idea to me. Just because it is documented doesn't mean changing it will not break people.
Why not change the documentation to say that podman on Window WSL2 can support multiple machines.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Someone interested in opening a Docs PR?

When I was implementing the Windows/WSL backend it was very much intentional to support running more than one, since the contract definition strongly suggests it should work (it's a bit unexpected that you can't - everything else works this way: processes, containers, vms etc), and when I was looking at the qemu impl alot of the code already supports it or appears to have intended to support it in the future, except for the mac address hardcoding which is also called out in comments as being a temporary limitation. (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/eac1e947ea4998bb4ee60137e41389fba6c10d48/pkg/machine/qemu/config.go#L89).    

It does open some interesting use-cases like the ability to live copy between machines. Although I admit it is a less common more advanced use case. 

On the connection issue, keep in mind that it's the same case whether we support multiple machines running at the same time or not, since we still support defining more than one, and you still have to switch connections to talk to the other instance (different keys, ports, etc) - even if the other isnt running.
It's definitely something to think about.  The upcoming `podman buildfarm` command would benefit a lot if the limitation of 1 running machine would be dropped.

i think this must be for WSL?  For hyperv, you cannot start more than one:

```
PS C:\Users\baude\go\src\github.com\containers\podman> .\bin\windows\podman.exe machine start foobar
Error: cannot start VM foobar. VM podman-machine-default is currently running or starting: only one VM can be active at a time
```

I'll keep digging for wsl

I have edited the title of this issue to more accurately reflect things.  It is true that podman machine using WSL can have multiple machines as explained by @n1hility .  No other provider currently supports this.  i am closing this issue now, but it can be reopened or start a new issue if this warrants more attention
